revision.text:
[[en:H. Byron Earhart]][[id:H. Byron Earhart]] {{short description|American historian (born 1935)}} {{infobox person |name=H. Byron Earhart |birth_date={{birth date and age|1935|1|7}} |birth_place={{ill|Aledo, Illinois|en|Aledo, Illinois|qid=Q576814|lt=Aledo, Illinois|1=Aledo, Illinois}}, U.S. |education={{ill|Knox College (Illinois)|en|Knox College (Illinois)|qid=Q669442|lt=Knox College|1=Knox College (Illinois)}}
{{ill|University of Chicago|en|University of Chicago|qid=Q131252|lt=University of Chicago|1=University of Chicago}} |occupation={{flatlist| *Historian *author }} |spouse={{marriage|Virginia Margaret Donaho|1956}} |children=3 }} '''H. Byron Earhart''' (born January 7, 1935) is an American historian, {{ill|Doctor of Philosophy|en|Doctor of Philosophy|qid=Q752297|lt=Ph.D|1=Doctor of Philosophy}}, and author, especially about [[:en:Japanese religion|Japanese religion]]s.{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Hi-xCwAAQBAJ&pg=PA348|title=Writers Directory|date=2016-03-05|publisher=Springer|isbn=978-1-349-03650-9|language=en}} == Life and studies == He was born on January 7, 1935, in {{ill|Aledo, Illinois|en|Aledo, Illinois|qid=Q576814|lt=Aledo, Illinois|1=Aledo, Illinois}}; son of Kenneth Harry and Mary ({{nee}} Haack) Earhart.{{Cite book|last=Evory|first=Ann|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=KlBgYZH570AC|page=148|title=Contemporary Authors|date=1979|publisher=Gale / Cengage Learning|isbn=978-0-8103-0040-8|language=en}} His father enlisted in the {{ill|United States Navy|en|United States Navy|qid=Q11220|lt=U.S. Navy|1=United States Navy}} in 1942 and served on the battleship {{ill|USS Missouri|en|USS Missouri|qid=Q2470317|lt=USS Missouri|1=USS Missouri}}. His grandparents and mother held a frozen food locker in {{ill|Havana, Illinois|en|Havana, Illinois|qid=Q575599|lt=Havana, Illinois|1=Havana, Illinois}}.{{Cite web|last=Moon|first=Jill|date=2020-08-19|title=Illinoisan's story a tribute to everyday Americans|url=https://www.thetelegraph.com/features/article/Illinoisan-8217-s-story-a-tribute-to-everyday-15496551.php|access-date=2020-11-19|website=[[The Telegraph (Alton, Illinois)|Alton Telegraph]]|language=en-US|archive-date=2022-01-10|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220110090230/https://www.thetelegraph.com/features/article/Illinoisan-8217-s-story-a-tribute-to-everyday-15496551.php|url-status=dead}} H. Byron Earhart married Virginia Margaret Donaho in 1956 and they had three children. Earhart attended {{ill|Knox College (Illinois)|en|Knox College (Illinois)|qid=Q669442|lt=Knox College|1=Knox College (Illinois)}} in {{ill|Galesburg, Illinois|en|Galesburg, Illinois|qid=Q782574|lt=Galesburg|1=Galesburg, Illinois}}, majoring in philosophy and religion. He enrolled at the {{ill|University of Chicago|en|University of Chicago|qid=Q131252|lt=University of Chicago|1=University of Chicago}} in a graduate program, got a {{ill|Fulbright Program|en|Fulbright Program|qid=Q253936|lt=Fulbright grant|1=Fulbright Program}} and went to Japan for three years of doctoral research. He studied under {{ill|Mircea Eliade|en|Mircea Eliade|qid=Q41590|lt=Mircea Eliade|1=Mircea Eliade}} and [[:en:Joseph M. Kitagawa|Joseph M. Kitagawa]] at the University of Chicago, where he received a doctorate in {{ill|History of religion|en|History of religion|qid=Q846742|lt=History of Religions|1=History of religion}}.{{Cite web|title=H. Byron Earhart|url=https://www.cengage.co.uk/author/h-byron-earhart/|access-date=2020-11-18|website=Cengage EMEA|language=en-GB}} == Career == He is a {{ill|professor emeritus|en|professor emeritus|qid=Q1240569|lt=professor emeritus|1=Professor emeritus}} in the Department of Comparative Religion at {{ill|Western Michigan University|en|Western Michigan University|qid=Q1546191|lt=Western Michigan University|1=Western Michigan University}}{{Cite web|title=H. Byron Earhart|url=https://wmich.edu/religion/directory/earhart|access-date=2020-11-18|website=Western Michigan University|language=en}} from which he received in 1981 a Distinguished Faculty Scholar Award.{{Cite web|date=October 15, 1981|title=Western news|url=https://scholarworks.wmich.edu/cgi/viewcontent.cgi?referer=https://www.google.com/&httpsredir=1&article=1972&context=western_news}} His textbook ''Japanese Religion: Unity and Diversity '' (1969) is considered a classic, through several editions, and "has remained one of the only treatments of Japanese religious history truly suitable for use in undergraduate classrooms".{{Cite journal|last=Thumas|first=Jonathan|date=2013-09-22|title=H. Byron Earhart, Religion in Japan: Unity and Diversity (fifth edition)|url=https://go.gale.com/ps/i.do?p=AONE&sw=w&issn=03041042&v=2.1&it=r&id=GALE%7CA352491099&sid=googleScholar&linkaccess=abs|journal=[[Japanese Journal of Religious Studies]]|language=en|volume=40|issue=2|pages=383–386|doi=10.18874/jjrs.40.2.2013.383-385|quote=It is no surprise that H. Byron Earhart's classic textbook, Japanese Religion, has remained one of the only treatments of Japanese religious history truly suitable for use in undergraduate classrooms. During its long publication history, Japanese Religion has, without equal, fulfilled and exceeded its role as a useful teaching material. Earhart has proved through various editions that his work remains relevant and indeed the foremost resource for those teaching introductory courses on Japanese and East Asian religions. This continues to be the case in the latest, fifth edition, aptly titled Religion in Japan.|doi-access=free}} == Bibliography (excerpts) == * ''Japanese Religion: Unity and Diversity'', 1969, and later editions,{{Cite journal|last=Reid|first=David|date=1982|title=Review Of: H. Byron Earhart, Japanese Religion: Unity and Diversity|url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/nfile/2255|journal=Japanese Journal of Religious Studies|volume=9|issue=4|pages=313–315|doi=10.18874/jjrs.9.4.1982.313-315|doi-access=free}}{{Cite journal|language=fr|last=Aubin|first=Françoise|date=1984|title=Earhart (Byron) Japanese Religion. Unity and Diversity|url=https://www.persee.fr/doc/assr_0335-5985_1984_num_57_2_2322_t1_0228_0000_4|journal=[[Archives de sciences sociales des religions]]|volume=57|issue=2|pages=228–229}}{{Cite web|last=Lee|first=Andrew|date=2013-11-30|title=Religion in Japan: Unity and Diversity|url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/culture/2013/11/30/books/book-reviews/religion-in-japan-unity-and-diversity/|access-date=2020-11-19|website=[[The Japan Times]]|language=en-US}} also as ''Religion in Japan: Unity and Diversity'' *''The new religions of Japan: a bibliography of Western-language materials''. xi, 96 pp. Tokyo: Sophia University, 1970.{{Cite journal|last=Blacker|first=Carmen|title=H. Byron Earhart: The new religions of Japan: a bibliography of Western-language materials. (Monumenta Nipponica Monograph Series.) xi, 96 pp. Tokyo: Sophia University, [1970].|url=https://www.cambridge.org/core/journals/bulletin-of-the-school-of-oriental-and-african-studies/article/h-byron-earhart-the-new-religions-of-japan-a-bibliography-of-westernlanguage-materials-monumenta-nipponica-monograph-series-xi-96-pp-tokyo-sophia-university-1970-575/9032D1F57848B5109455DC0378D290D0|journal=[[Bulletin of the School of Oriental and African Studies]]|language=en|volume=34|issue=3|date=1971|pages=679|doi=10.1017/S0041977X00129428|s2cid=162313830 |issn=1474-0699}}{{Cite journal|last=Ellwood|first=Robert S.|date=1971|title=Review of The New Religions of Japan: A Bibliography of Western-Language Materials|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/1461684|journal=[[Journal of the American Academy of Religion]]|volume=39|issue=1|pages=89–92|doi=10.1093/jaarel/XXXIX.1.89|jstor=1461684|issn=0002-7189}} University of Michigan, 1983, pp. 213.{{Cite journal|last=Hardacre|first=Helen|date=1984-08-01|title=The New Religions of Japan: A Bibliography of Western-Language Materials. H. Byron Earhart|url=https://www.journals.uchicago.edu/doi/abs/10.1086/462979|journal=[[History of Religions (journal)|History of Religions]]|volume=24|issue=1|pages=89–90|doi=10.1086/462979|issn=0018-2710}}{{Cite journal|last=Reid|first=David|date=Mar 1, 1984|title=The new religions of Japan: A bibliography of Western-language materials, 2nd edition (Book Review)|url=https://www.proquest.com/openview/f158776c91a2b90667a84780262d4760|journal=Japanese Journal of Religious Studies|doi=10.18874/jjrs.11.1.1984.95-96|doi-access=free}} * {{cite book|title=Religion in the Japanese Experience: Sources and Interpretations|url-access=registration|url=https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000earh|year=1974}} 1997 * ''Religions of Japan: Many Traditions Within One Sacred Way '', 1984, 1998 * ''Religious Traditions of the World: A Journey Through Africa, Mesoamerica, North America, Judaism, Christianity, Islam'', 1993 * ''Mount Fuji: Icon of Japan'', Columbia: The University of South Carolina Press, 2011{{Cite web|last=Mansfield|first=Stephen|title=Fuji-san: reflections on Japan's iconic mother mountain|url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/culture/2012/02/26/books/book-reviews/fuji-san-reflections-on-japans-iconic-mother-mountain/|date=Feb 26, 2012|website=The Japan Times|language=en-US}}{{Cite journal|last=MacWilliams|first=Mark|title=Mount Fuji: Icon of Japan. By H. Byron Earhart. Columbia: The University of South Carolina Press, 2011. Pp. 239.|url=https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/abs/10.1111/rsr.12185_2|journal=[[Religious Studies Review]]|language=en|volume=40|issue=4|pages=232|date=December 2014|doi=10.1111/rsr.12185_2|issn=1748-0922}} * {{Cite book|title=The World War II Homefront in Havana, Illinois: At Grandma's House|date=2020|publisher=Southern Illinois University Press|isbn=978-0-8093-7007-8|language=en}} == References == {{reflist}} ==External links== * [https://wmich.edu/religion/directory/earhart H. Byron Earhart, Western Michigan University] [[en:H. Byron Earhart]] [[id:H. Byron Earhart]] {{wikidata link|Q32539204}} [[Category:1935 births]] [[Category:21st-century American historians]] [[Category:American historians of religion]] [[Category:American male non-fiction writers]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
